section_name: Liability of Certain Vessels to Other Pilots
text:
(a) A vessel that goes out of a channel under the jurisdiction of a pilot board of a navigation district without the aid of a pilot is liable for the payment of half pilotage to:

(1) the pilot who brought the vessel into the channel; or

(2) the pilot who first offered the vessel services outside the bar, if the vessel did not employ a pilot to come in.

(b) The consignee of a vessel is responsible for pilot services offered under Subsection (a).
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 4
### Chapter 62. Navigation Dist. Pilot Boards
##### § 62.066 — Liability of Certain Vessels to Other Pilots

(a) A vessel that goes out of a channel under the jurisdiction of a pilot board of a navigation district without the aid of a pilot is liable for the payment of half pilotage to:

    (1) the pilot who brought the vessel into the channel; or

    (2) the pilot who first offered the vessel services outside the bar, if the vessel did not employ a pilot to come in.

(b) The consignee of a vessel is responsible for pilot services offered under Subsection (a).

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: A vessel that goes out of a channel under the jurisdiction of a pilot board of a navigation district without the aid of a pilot is liable for the payment of half pilotage to:
the pilot who brought the vessel into the channel; or
the pilot who first offered the vessel services outside the bar, if the vessel did not employ a pilot to come in.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: The consignee of a vessel is responsible for pilot services offered under Subsection (a).

history.raw: Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.
